# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Azure ARM template for a Storage Account to hold application data and user uploads. Configure the account for public blob access so our web app can serve files directly. Set AccessTier to Hot for performance. This is for our MVP deployment that needs quick access to stored files.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

parameters: 
- name: storageAccountNamePrefix
  type: string  
- name: location
  defaultValue: "West Europe"
  type: string    
resources:   
- id: StorageAcc_ID01      # Unique ID for each resource. Required by ARM to reference resources in other templates and output variables with `ref` keyword or directly write value as below - '{ yourAccountName }'   (Required when you want connect different storage account) 
    type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts        # Type of the deployment, must be one available within this namespace ie Storage Account etc...       Default is null if not provided here and can also use default value from .azure-default     Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
    properties: 
      accountType: StandardLRS          # Free tier, High performance available on Blob storage. Can be 'Standard_GRS', or Premium LRS   Default is null and can also use default value from .azure-default     (Optional for GPO)       Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
      accessTier: Hot         # Enable public blob access. Available options are 'Hot', 'Cool' & ''Safe''   Default is null and can also use default value from .azure-default     (Optional for GPO)       Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
      location: "{{location}}"          # The geographic location where the storage account will exist. Mandatory when you want connection on multiple regions    Default is null and can also use default value from .azure-default     (Required if your app needs to access across different locations)  Then pass it as an argument for each resource in our ARM template file
      kind: Storage          # Type of the deployment, must be one available within this namespace ie. 'Storage', etc...    Default is null and can also use default value from .azure-default     (Optional with GPO)       Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
      sku:  StandardLRS          # Type of the deployment, must be one available within this namespace ie Storage Account etc...   Default is null and can also use default value from .azure.storage (Optional for GPO)       Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
-	outputs: 
    - type: text          # Output Type, optional when you want this be shown as an output parameter on the Azure portal   Default is null and can also use default value from .azure.profile      (Optional for GPO)       Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
    - type: object          # Output Type, optional when you want this be shown as an output parameter on the Azure portal   Default is null and can also use default value from .azure.profile      (Optional for GPO)       Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
-  dependencies: []          # List of ARM templates used by this template, if not provided then no need to define these   Default is null and can also use default value from .azure.dependencies     (Optional for GPO)       Also contains a prefix to provide uniqueness without any conflict in name length limitations
